Practising English isn't something that can be done within one or two day. You need to put time and effort on it. It is harsh, but if you really want improvement, work hard. Neither me have very good performance on the usage of English, so work hard together dude.
How to effectively remember more vocab? I often forget what I have learnt.

quote.msg: Practising English isn't something that can be done within one or two day. You need to put time and effort on it. It is harsh, but if you really want improvement, work hard. Neither me have very good performance on the usage of English, so work hard together dude.

replies.msg: Very well said. Indeed, the more often you use English, the faster you get more comfortable with it. Once you get more confident with a language, you would then find it fun and enjoy using it. It also motivates you to further improve yourself.

replies.msg: You can start from reading English newspaper or books? Go on a English tv channel ? Speak to someone who enjoy practicing English as well? Or even order food with English in restaurants? I still remember when I was a kid, my English teacher bought me to a McDonald’s and made me to order in English. I ordered a McNugget kid meal. And I still remember this. You can start doing this little thing,although it might sound a little bit weird. But well, that literally cost you nothing tho, right? You eat McDonald’s anyway I guess? Haha

replies.msg: A language is a knowledge that benefits your life as a whole. While it may not seem important to your job for now, English as a common language exists in tons of media, articles, speeches which cannot be easily translated to Chinese. When you meet a new friend outside of HK, English might be a possible way of communication, and that leads to you to a new experience and brings you to a new culture
